heading: Determination of issue price in the case of certain debt instruments issued for property
label: 1274 Determination of issue price in the case of certain debt instruments issued for property
notes: Amendments2018—Subsec. (b)(3)(B)(i). substituted “section 6662(d)(2)(C)(ii)” for “section 6662(d)(2)(C)(iii)”.Pub. L. 115–1411997—Subsec. Transitional Rule for Purposes of Imputed Interest RulesProvisions respecting treatment of debt instruments received in exchange for property, relating to special rules for sales after , and before , general rule for assumptions of loans, exception for assumptions of loans made on or before , and exception for assumptions of loans with respect to certain property, see section 44(b)(4)–(7) of , as amended, set out as an Effective Date note under .Dec. 31, 1984July 1, 1985Oct. 15, 1984Pub. L. 98–369section 1271 of this title

children.text: where there is adequate stated interest, the stated principal amount, or
in any other case, the imputed principal amount.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (3), the imputed principal amount of any debt instrument shall be equal to the sum of the present values of all payments due under such debt instrument.

children.label: A
children.text: as of the date of the sale or exchange, and

children.label: B
children.text: by using a discount rate equal to the applicable Federal rate, compounded semiannually.

children.heading: Fair market value rule in potentially abusive situations
children.label: 3 Fair market value rule in potentially abusive situations

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of any potentially abusive situation, the imputed principal amount of any debt instrument received in exchange for property shall be the fair market value of such property adjusted to take into account other consideration involved in the transaction.

children.label: i
children.text: a tax shelter (as defined in section 6662(d)(2)(C)(ii)), and

children.label: I
children.text: recent sales transactions,

children.label: II
children.text: nonrecourse financing,

children.label: III
children.text: financing with a term in excess of the economic life of the property, or

children.label: IV
children.text: other circumstances,

children.heading: Debt instruments to which section applies
children.label: c Debt instruments to which section applies
i
children.text: where there is adequate stated interest, the stated principal amount, or

children.label: ii
children.text: in any other case, the imputed principal amount of such debt instrument determined under subsection (b), and

children.label: B
children.text: some or all of the payments due under such debt instrument are due more than 6 months after the date of such sale or exchange.

children.heading: Adequate stated interest
children.label: 2 Adequate stated interest
children.text: For purposes of this section, there is adequate stated interest with respect to any debt instrument if the stated principal amount for such debt instrument is less than or equal to the imputed principal amount of such debt instrument determined under subsection (b).

children.heading: Sales for $1,000,000 or less of farms by individuals or small businesses
children.label: A Sales for $1,000,000 or less of farms by individuals or small businesses
I
children.text: by an individual, estate, or testamentary trust,

children.label: II
children.text: by a corporation which as of the date of the sale or exchange is a small business corporation (as defined in section 1244(c)(3)), or

children.label: III
children.text: by a partnership which as of the date of the sale or exchange meets requirements similar to those of section 1244(c)(3).

children.heading: $1,000,000 limitation
children.label: ii $1,000,000 limitation
children.text: Clause (i) shall apply only if it can be determined at the time of the sale or exchange that the sales price cannot exceed $1,000,000. For purposes of the preceding sentence, all sales and exchanges which are part of the same transaction (or a series of related transactions) shall be treated as 1 sale or exchange.

children.heading: Sales of principal residences
children.label: B Sales of principal residences
children.text: Any debt instrument arising from the sale or exchange by an individual of his principal residence (within the meaning of section 121).

children.heading: Sales involving total payments of $250,000 or less
children.label: C Sales involving total payments of $250,000 or less
I
children.text: the aggregate amount of the payments due under such debt instrument and all other debt instruments received as consideration for the sale or exchange, and

children.label: II
children.text: the aggregate amount of any other consideration to be received for the sale or exchange.

children.heading: Consideration other than debt instrument taken into account at fair market value
children.label: ii Consideration other than debt instrument taken into account at fair market value
children.text: For purposes of clause (i), any consideration (other than a debt instrument) shall be taken into account at its fair market value.

children.heading: Aggregation of transactions
children.label: iii Aggregation of transactions
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, all sales and exchanges which are part of the same transaction (or a series of related transactions) shall be treated as 1 sale or exchange.

children.heading: Debt instruments which are publicly traded or issued for publicly traded property
children.label: D Debt instruments which are publicly traded or issued for publicly traded property
children.text: Any debt instrument to which section 1273(b)(3) applies.

children.heading: Certain sales of patents
children.label: E Certain sales of patents
children.text: In the case of any transfer described in section 1235(a) (relating to sale or exchange of patents), any amount contingent on the productivity, use, or disposition of the property transferred.

children.heading: Sales or exchanges to which section 483(e) applies
children.label: F Sales or exchanges to which section 483(e) applies
children.text: Any debt instrument to the extent section 483(e) (relating to certain land transfers between related persons) applies to such instrument.

children.label: A
children.text: in connection with the sale or exchange of property, assumes any debt instrument, or

children.label: B
children.text: acquires any property subject to any debt instrument,

children.heading: Determination of applicable Federal rate
children.label: d Determination of applicable Federal rate

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of adebt instrumentwith a term of:The applicable Federalrate is:Not over 3 yearsThe Federal short-term rate.Over 3 years but not over 9 yearsThe Federal mid-term rate.Over 9 yearsThe Federal long-term rate.

children.heading: Determination of rates
children.label: B Determination of rates
children.text: During each calendar month, the Secretary shall determine the Federal short-term rate, mid-term rate, and long-term rate which shall apply during the following calendar month.

children.heading: Federal rate for any calendar month
children.label: C Federal rate for any calendar month

children.heading: Federal short-term rate
children.label: i Federal short-term rate
children.text: The Federal short-term rate shall be the rate determined by the Secretary based on the average market yield (during any 1-month period selected by the Secretary and ending in the calendar month in which the determination is made) on outstanding marketable obligations of the United States with remaining periods to maturity of 3 years or less.

children.heading: Federal mid-term and long-term rates
children.label: ii Federal mid-term and long-term rates
children.text: The Federal mid-term and long-term rate shall be determined in accordance with the principles of clause (i).

children.heading: Lower rate permitted in certain cases
children.label: D Lower rate permitted in certain cases
children.text: The Secretary may by regulations permit a rate to be used with respect to any debt instrument which is lower than the applicable Federal rate if the taxpayer establishes to the satisfaction of the Secretary that such lower rate is based on the same principles as the applicable Federal rate and is appropriate for the term of such instrument.

children.heading: Lowest 3-month rate applicable to any sale or exchange
children.label: 2 Lowest 3-month rate applicable to any sale or exchange

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of any sale or exchange, the applicable Federal rate shall be the lowest 3-month rate.

children.heading: Lowest 3-month rate
children.label: B Lowest 3-month rate
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the term “lowest 3-month rate” means the lowest of the applicable Federal rates in effect for any month in the 3-calendar-month period ending with the 1st calendar month in which there is a binding contract in writing for such sale or exchange.

children.heading: Term of debt instrument
children.label: 3 Term of debt instrument
children.text: In determining the term of a debt instrument for purposes of this subsection, under regulations prescribed by the Secretary, there shall be taken into account options to renew or extend.

children.heading: 110 Percent rate where sale-leaseback involved
children.label: e 110 Percent rate where sale-leaseback involved

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In the case of any debt instrument to which this subsection applies, the discount rate used under subsection (b)(2)(B) or section 483(b) shall be 110 percent of the applicable Federal rate, compounded semiannually.

children.heading: Lower discount rates shall not apply
children.label: 2 Lower discount rates shall not apply
children.text: Section 1274A shall not apply to any debt instrument to which this subsection applies.

children.heading: Debt instruments to which this subsection applies
children.label: 3 Debt instruments to which this subsection applies
children.text: This subsection shall apply to any debt instrument given in consideration for the sale or exchange of any property if, pursuant to a plan, the transferor or any related person leases a portion of such property after such sale or exchange.
